title: Volvo Cars rapportrusar: "Marknaden volatil"
summary_md:
# Volvo Cars rapportrusar: "Marknaden volatil"

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-04-29  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Volvo Cars CEO Håkan Samuelsson discusses Q1 results that beat profitability expectations, with revenue of SEK 72.6bn and operating profit of SEK 1.6bn (2.2% margin). The company reaffirms its target to return to volume growth in 2026, driven by cost reductions (SEK 5bn material savings program plus continued restrictive indirect costs) and electrification via the new mid-class SUV EX60, whose orders are exceeding internal forecasts. Management expresses confidence in the new model's software readiness, having moved central architecture challenges behind them.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 VOLCAR-B — Volvo Cars | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Q1 profitability beat expectations; reaffirmed 2026 volume growth; SEK 5bn cost program; new EX60 SUV orders exceeding internal forecasts amid fast EU EV growth (+26% Q1).

#### Reasoning

CEO Samuelsson signals operational discipline: SEK 8bn savings delivered last year, SEK 5bn material savings this year via Geely synergies, plus restrictive indirect costs. Growth thesis hinges on electrification and the new EX60 mid-class SUV (~40k units this year), targeting the largest market segment Volvo previously lacked. Software now mature (shared X90/EX90 stack), reducing launch risk. EU EV sales up 26% in Q1 supports the transition bet.

#### Sell Triggers

- if EX60 ramp-up faces production delays
- if EV demand in Europe slows
- if 2026 volume growth target is missed
- if cost savings program underdelivers

> *"Det är bättre än väntat och man upprepar dessutom sitt mål om att återgå till volymtillväxt under 2026."*

---
